title: Does Rebuild Actually Offer Anything New
display_title: Does Rebuild Actually Offer Anything New
wikitext:
[]: Does Rebuild Actually Offer Anything New? [SPOILERS]             Quote Post by J_Faulkner » Sun Dec 27, 2009 1:55 pm I've been reading some of the plot behind 1.0 and 2.0, and I've got to question: does Rebuild actually offer anything new? By this, I don't mean new characters, pretty new animation and so forth, but underlying messages. For example, one of the underlying messages in the original series and EoE concerns how shutting yourself off from examining yourself can contribute to depression. But what about Rebuild? Do we get anything over and above the messages already present in the original series and EoE?

[]:             Quote Post by Sailor Star Dust » Sun Dec 27, 2009 2:31 pm So far the films seem to be plot driven instead of having some underlying message, so I haven't noticed much yet outside of Misato believing in Shinji/wanting Shinji to believe in himself near the end of 1.0. On the flip side in 2.0, when Shinji (with Eva-01's help) is absorbed into the Eva and tries to save Rei, disregarding the rest of the world and humanity... He's having a selfish EoE 3I moment (although it's not portrayed as such a bitter context of say, wanting the world to die, he's has already given up on Nerv due to what happened to Asuka earlier that day) with only caring for his own needs and such. But yeah, I guess it can be said the message of believing in yourself is subverted with 2.0 since it's at the cost of Eva-01/Rei nearly causing 3rd Impact. Only time will tell what Rebuild's underlying message will be, I'd say.

[]:             Quote Post by Joseph the PRPD » Sun Dec 27, 2009 2:36 pm I agree with SSD's post but if you're looking for some deep message or whatever in Rebuild as a whole we might not see it until 3.0/4.0 but I don't think any thing really "deep" is there.

[]:             Quote Post by honsou » Sun Dec 27, 2009 2:48 pm No and that's kinda the point of Rebuild. In the same way that Evangelion series was a deconstruction of the Mecha genre, Rebuild seems to be a reconstruction of the genre. This isn't done though homage but changing things as so character development would happen in a more traditional way. Having Shinji grow mental strength and a bit of a backbone; instead of stunting his grow at any turn (The Leliel fight taking any bit of confident away from Shinji for example). Also the only new character; Mari seems like she was transported directly from a super robot show in the 70s (I'm going to go with Getter Robo). In this sense Rebuild is basically saying "old is new again"

[]: Re: Does Rebuild Actually Offer Anything New? [SPOILERS]             Quote Post by Xard » Sun Dec 27, 2009 3:33 pm J_Faulkner wrote: I've been reading some of the plot behind 1.0 and 2.0, and I've got to question: does Rebuild actually offer anything new? By this, I don't mean new characters, pretty new animation and so forth, but underlying messages. For example, one of the underlying messages in the original series and EoE concerns how shutting yourself off from examining yourself can contribute to depression. But what about Rebuild? Do we get anything over and above the messages already present in the original series and EoE?

[]:             Quote Post by Heromaster111 » Sun Dec 27, 2009 3:36 pm could just watch them it's not like it's that long of an endeavor.

[]:             Quote Post by jayngfet » Sun Dec 27, 2009 4:07 pm honsou wrote: No and that's kinda the point of Rebuild. In the same way that Evangelion series was a deconstruction of the Mecha genre, Rebuild seems to be a reconstruction of the genre. This isn't done though homage but changing things as so character development would happen in a more traditional way. Having Shinji grow mental strength and a bit of a backbone; instead of stunting his grow at any turn (The Leliel fight taking any bit of confident away from Shinji for example). Also the only new character; Mari seems like she was transported directly from a super robot show in the 70s (I'm going to go with Getter Robo). In this sense Rebuild is basically saying "old is new again"

[]: Re: Does Rebuild Actually Offer Anything New? [SPOILERS]             Quote Post by Lucretius » Sun Dec 27, 2009 5:03 pm J_Faulkner wrote: new characters, pretty new animation

[]: Re: Does Rebuild Actually Offer Anything New? [SPOILERS]             Quote Post by esselfortium » Sun Dec 27, 2009 5:03 pm J_Faulkner wrote: I've been reading some of the plot behind 1.0 and 2.0, and I've got to question: does Rebuild actually offer anything new?

[]:             Quote Post by Legendary » Sun Dec 27, 2009 7:56 pm Impossible to tell. 1.0 was Introduction. 2.0 was Action. If the Descent phase occurs, that'll be 3.0.

[]: Re: Does Rebuild Actually Offer Anything New? [SPOILERS]             Quote Post by SaltyJoe » Mon Dec 28, 2009 2:14 am J_Faulkner wrote: I've been reading some of the plot behind 1.0 and 2.0, and I've got to question: does Rebuild actually offer anything new?

[]:             Quote Post by AuraTwilight » Mon Dec 28, 2009 4:33 am This is a pointless question. How can you properly evaluate the higher message or theme of an incomplete work? You don't know if any Aesop being given is going to be subverted, deconstructed, affirmed, or what.

[]:             Quote Post by The Eva Monkey » Mon Dec 28, 2009 8:12 am J_Faulkner wrote: does Rebuild actually offer anything new?

[]:             Quote Post by konfunoodle » Mon Dec 28, 2009 9:56 pm Let's not also forget that Asuka was the pilot of Eva-03 instead of Toji, that could flip Asuka's character out of porportion for the rest of the movies.Maybe she got her mind raped that whole time and her mind is still contaminated. Who knows?

[]:             Quote Post by Fallingtogrey » Tue Dec 29, 2009 8:42 pm Kind of.
